message:
fix(tools): canonicalize file paths in EditTool/WriteTool and harden GrepTool match iteration

Three targeted fixes to the tool implementations:

EditTool path resolution:
- Wrap resolve_read_path() output with crate::extensions::safe_canonicalize()
  to resolve symlinks and normalize relative path components (e.g., "../")
  before checking file existence and applying edits. Without this, edits
  targeting symlinked paths or paths with ".." segments could fail the
  existence check or write to the wrong location.

WriteTool path resolution:
- Apply the same safe_canonicalize() treatment to resolve_path() in
  WriteTool, ensuring consistent path normalization between the read
  and write tool implementations. This prevents the case where ReadTool
  sees a file at its canonical path but WriteTool fails to find it at
  a non-canonical equivalent.

GrepTool match iteration:
- Replace unwrap() on matches_by_file.remove() with a let-else continue
  pattern. During concurrent file modifications, a file path present in
  file_order may have had its matches already consumed or removed from
  matches_by_file. The unwrap() would panic; the new pattern gracefully
  skips the missing entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
